results.title: ElevenLabs Avatars Transform Enterprise Video
results.content:
Publication Date: June 12, 2026

ElevenLabs has launched Avatars in ElevenCreative, enabling users to generate talking-head videos with integrated voice and lip-sync in a single workflow ( "Introducing Avatars in ElevenCreative: AI Talking Video"). This move streamlines content creation for marketers and educators, raising the stakes for established video AI vendors and forcing enterprises to reconsider their approach to brand identity and localization.

## What is Covered in this Article [...] The News: ElevenLabs has introduced Avatars in ElevenCreative, a new feature that lets users generate talking-head videos by combining ElevenLabs' proprietary speech models with advanced lip-syncing models in a single interface ( "Introducing Avatars in ElevenCreative: AI Talking Video"). Users can create persistent avatars from images or text prompts, maintain consistent identities across videos, and use a curated library for immediate use. The Avatars workflow eliminates the need for separate audio exports or third-party tools, delivering tighter voice-to-video synchronization. With the new Avatar node in Flows, content teams can automate video production pipelines, enabling batch creation of localized or variant content for marketing, education, or product explainers ( "Introducing [...] Analyst Directory

# What's New

   Create talking-head videos from a script, voice, and avatar in a single experience

   Generate persistent avatars from your own photos and reuse them across projects

   Create multiple styles with different outfits, camera angles, and environments while maintaining the same identity

   Use Text to Speech directly within the avatar workflow

   Access a library of ready-to-use avatars and styles

   Use the Avatar node in Flows to generate video variations at scale

ElevenLabs has introduced Avatars in ElevenCreative, allowing users to create studio-quality talking videos from a script, voice, and AI avatar. Could AI avatars replace traditional talking-head video production for creators and businesses?

results.title: 15 Best AI Avatar Generators in 2026: Compared by Use Case
results.content:
### 6 Colossyan – AI Avatars for E‑Learning and Compliance

Colossyan supports SCORM‑compliant courses and structured learning videos.

Best for: Compliance and training programs

Limitations: Limited emotional expression in avatars

### 7 Speechify – Audio‑First AI Avatars

Speechify focuses on voice and audio workflows, with avatar features layered on top.

Best for: Fast text‑to‑video narration

Limitations: Less variety in long‑form visual content

### 8 Tavus – Personalized AI Digital Twins

Tavus allows users to create AI versions of themselves for conversational videos.

Best for: Personalized sales and engagement

Limitations: Higher cost for scaled usage

### 9 Runway – Creative and Artistic AI Video Generation [...] Best for: Asset sorting and lightweight visuals

Limitations: No advanced video avatars

### 13 AI Ease – Mobile AI Avatar and Headshot Tool

AI Ease provides fast avatar and headshot generation on mobile devices.

Best for: Professional profile images

Limitations: Not built for video production

### 14 Dawn – Fun Mobile Avatar App

Dawn focuses on artistic and playful avatar styles from selfies.

Best for: Personal and creative use

Limitations: Not suitable for business or training videos

### 15 HeyGen – AI Avatars for Fast Presentation and Social Videos [...] | VEED | Small teams needing edit + light avatars | General editing + quick avatar features | Avatar depth vs specialized platforms |
| Colossyan | E‑learning/compliance | SCORM‑oriented learning workflows | Limited emotional range in avatars |
| Speechify | Fast narration‑first workflows | Voice/audio strength | Less long‑form visual depth |
| Tavus | Personalized “digital twin” outreach | Personalization at the individual level | Cost at scale |
| Runway | Creative/experimental video | Creative control | Not optimized for corporate training systems |
| Picsart | Casual/social creative | Fun, lightweight outputs | Not a professional business video stack |
| Canva | Quick design‑led videos | Speed within design ecosystem | Limited avatar realism/interaction |

results.title: Lenovo's AI solutions power 2026 FIFA World Cup with 3D avatars and more | Fox Business
results.content:
Lionel Messi kisses the trophy

Argentina forward Lionel Messi (10) kisses the World Cup Trophy after winning the 2022 World Cup final against France at Lusail Stadium.  (Yukihito Taguchi/USA Today Sports / IMAGN)

There will be 3D player avatars produced at the tournament using 3D assets and Advanced GenAI technology, which will help support the efficiency of the decision-making process made by referees during matches. In just six seconds, a player will step into a circle filled with cameras that scan the player's body to build the 3D asset, which will be used in key situations. [...] GET FOX BUSINESS ON THE GO BY CLICKING HERE

To enhance Lenovo’s messaging about how it's impacting the FIFA World Cup, as well as the FIFA Women’s World Cup in 2027, the brand launched its "Maximum David" campaign with legend David Beckham. The campaign highlights how AI-driven technology elevates the creativity, performance and impact of Beckham and ultimately how it will impact all those participating in the tournament.

From teams looking into data for an advantage to fans getting never-before-seen views of the world’s greatest show on the pitch, Lenovo and FIFA are working side by side to deliver the game like it’s never been seen on a global stage.

It will also start soon. The first World Cup match will be played June 11. [...] For example, during an offside replay in a match, 3D animations will appear on the screen, providing a greater visual contextualization for fans watching at home and in the stadium. The digital avatars will replicate the individual physical dimensions of the players competing.

"AI-enabled 3D avatars mark a major step forward in how officiating technology supports accuracy and transparency," FIFA Secretary General Mattias Grafström said in a statement. "By combining precise player data with advanced visualization, this innovation strengthens confidence in key decisions and brings fans closer to the process than ever before."
